name: BeeAI Framework
tagline: Build production AI agents in Python or TypeScript with shared components
metaDescription: BeeAI Framework is an open-source toolkit for building production agents and multi-agent systems in Python or TypeScript, with tools, memory, RAG, and any LLM.
overview: BeeAI Framework is an open-source toolkit for building autonomous agents and multi-agent systems. It ships in both Python and TypeScript and shares a common set of components across the two languages, so teams can stay on the stack they already use.

It is aimed at developers who need to wire LLMs to tools, memory, and other agents in real applications rather than one-off scripts. The Backend module gives a single interface to many LLM providers, and built-in tools (web search, weather, code execution) and RAG support cover common agent needs.

As a general agent framework, it sits alongside other orchestration libraries: you define agents, give them tools and rules, and let them reason, act, and hand work off to each other. The experimental Requirement Agent adds rules that constrain agent behavior for more predictable runs across different models.
features: Write agents in Python or TypeScript with a shared component model across both languages
Backend module connects to many LLM providers through one unified interface
Built-in tools for web search, weather, and code execution, plus support for custom tools
Requirement Agent lets you set rules an agent must follow for more predictable behavior
RAG module for retrieval-augmented generation with vector stores and document processing
Workflows, memory, and Mustache-based prompt templates for multi-agent systems

gettingStarted.intro: Install the package for your language, then create an agent that uses a chat model and tools. The example below uses the Python Requirement Agent.

steps.heading: Install (Python)
steps.body: Install the Python package from PyPI.

steps.heading: Install (TypeScript)
steps.body: Or install the TypeScript package from npm.

steps.heading: Create a Requirement Agent
steps.body: Give the agent a chat model and tools. ChatModel.from_name selects an LLM provider; this example uses a local Ollama model.
steps.code:
import asyncio

from beeai_framework.agents.requirement import RequirementAgent
from beeai_framework.backend import ChatModel
from beeai_framework.tools.search.wikipedia import WikipediaTool
from beeai_framework.tools.think import ThinkTool


async def main() -> None:
    agent = RequirementAgent(
        llm=ChatModel.from_name("ollama:granite3.3:8b"),
        tools=[ThinkTool(), WikipediaTool()],
        role="Knowledge Specialist",
        instructions="Provide answers to general questions about the world.",
    )
    response = await agent.run("Tell me one famous landmark in Rome.")
    print("Agent:", response.last_message.text)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    asyncio.run(main())

useCases: Build a multi-agent system where a main agent hands tasks off to specialist agents
Connect an agent to multiple LLM providers behind one interface and swap models without rewriting code
Add retrieval-augmented generation over your own documents using the RAG module
Constrain agent behavior with rules so runs stay predictable across different models
